message.content:
I'm investigating a Kazakhstan ЕНТ test-prep app to wire a new "question bank" into the student's node-test start flow. There are TWO frontends and I need to know which is the REAL production student app, and map the exact start-a-node-test flow in it.

The two frontends:
1. `/Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/client` — a legacy combined SPA (React+Vite+Tailwind, axios). Look for `client/src/pages/**/KnowledgeMapPage*` and a `handleStart` function.
2. `/Users/madiever/edu-pwa/packages/edu-app` — a yarn-monorepo student PWA (React, FSD architecture, RTK Query, MUI). This is suspected to be the real production student app (deployed on Render).

I need you to be VERY THOROUGH and answer these questions with concrete file:line citations:

A. **Which frontend is the production student app?** Check for signals: deploy config (look at `/Users/madiever/edu-pwa/render.yaml`, any nginx config in GenTestMVP, package.json build scripts, README), recency of changes (git log on the relevant dirs), whether one looks abandoned/legacy. State your confidence.

B. **In edu-pwa/packages/edu-app**, find the "knowledge map" / roadmap screen where a student starts a test on a roadmap NODE (a KTP topic). Find:
   - The page/component file and the handler that fires when the student clicks "start"/"начать" on a node.
   - Which API endpoint / RTK Query hook it calls to GENERATE the node test (look for things like `node-bank`, `generateNodeTest`, `roadmap`, `generate`). Quote the endpoint path and the request body.
   - How the resulting test is navigated to / rendered (route, component).

C. **Roadmap зачёт (completion credit):** How does a node get marked as "mastered"/"освоен" after the student finishes a test? Look for `roadmapSessionId`, `saveRoadmapContext`, `roadmapContext`, or anything that ties a finished test back to the roadmap node so progress advances. Find both the client-side wiring (what gets sent on test start AND on test finish/submit) and any sessionStorage/state persistence.

D. **Same in the legacy GenTestMVP/client** (briefly): find `KnowledgeMapPage.handleStart` and what endpoint it calls, just enough to confirm whether it's the same or different flow, so I know if it's truly legacy/unused.

Do NOT modify any files. Return a structured report: (1) which frontend is production + confidence, (2) the edu-app node-test start flow with file:line + endpoint + body, (3) the roadmap зачёт mechanism with file:line, (4) brief note on the legacy client flow. Be precise — I will write code against your findings.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- figma:figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component mapping, design-to-code translation, or asks to create/update .figma.ts or .figma.js files.
- figma:figma-create-new-file: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `create_new_file` tool call. NEVER call `create_new_file` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user wants a new blank Figma file — a new design, FigJam, or Slides file — or when you need a fresh file before calling `use_figma`. Usage — /figma-create-new-file [editorType] [fileName] (e.g. /figma-create-new-file figjam My Whiteboard, /figma-create-new-file slides Q3 Review)
- figma:figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma', 'create in Figma from code', 'push page to Figma', 'take this app/page and build it in Figma', 'create a screen', 'build a landing page in Figma', 'update the Figma screen to match code', 'convert this modal/dialog/drawer/panel to Figma'. This is the preferred workflow skill whenever the user wants to build or update a full page, modal, dialog, drawer, sidebar, panel, or any composed multi-section view in Figma from code or a description. Discovers design system components, variables, and styles from Code Connect files, existing screens, and library search, then imports them and assembles views incrementally section-by-section using design system tokens instead of hardcoded values.
- figma:figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. NEVER call `generate_diagram` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user asks to create, generate, draw, render, sketch, or build a diagram — flowchart, architecture diagram, sequence diagram, ERD or entity-relationship diagram, state diagram or state machine, gantt chart, or timeline. Also trigger when the user mentions Mermaid syntax or wants a system architecture, decision tree, dependency graph, API call flow, auth handshake, schema, or pipeline visualized in FigJam. Routes to type-specific guidance, sets universal Mermaid constraints, and tells you when to use a different diagram type or skip the tool entirely (mindmaps, pie charts, class diagrams, etc.).
- figma:figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, create individual components with proper variant sets and variable bindings, set up theming (light/dark modes), document foundations, or reconcile gaps between code and Figma. Also use when the user asks to create or generate any component in Figma — even a single one — since components require proper variable foundations, variant states, and design token bindings to be production-quality. This skill teaches WHAT to build and in WHAT ORDER — it complements the `figma-use` skill which teaches HOW to call the Plugin API. Both skills should be loaded together.
- figma:figma-implement-motion: Translates Figma motion and animations into production-ready application code. Use when implementing animation/motion from a Figma design — user mentions "implement this motion", "add animation from Figma", "animate this component", provides a Figma URL whose node is animated, or when `get_design_context` returns motion data or instructs you to call `get_motion_context`.
- figma:figma-swiftui: SwiftUI ↔ Figma translation. Use whenever the user mentions Swift, SwiftUI, iOS, iPhone, or iPad — in EITHER direction — translating a Figma design into SwiftUI (design → code), or pushing SwiftUI views / screens / tokens back into a Figma file (code → design). Triggers on phrases like 'implement this Figma design in SwiftUI', 'build this screen in Swift', 'push this SwiftUI view to Figma', 'mirror my Swift code in a Figma file', or whenever a Figma URL appears alongside `.swift` files / an `.xcodeproj`. Routes to a direction-specific reference doc; loads alongside `figma-use` for the code → design path.
- figma:figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Skipping it causes common, hard-to-debug failures. Trigger whenever the user wants to perform a write action or a unique read action that requires JavaScript execution in the Figma file context — e.g. create/edit/delete nodes, set up variables or tokens, build components and variants, modify auto-layout or fills, bind variables to properties, or inspect file structure programmatically.
- figma:figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- figma:figma-use-motion: Motion / animation context for the `use_figma` MCP tool — animating Figma nodes via manual keyframes, animation styles, easing, and timeline duration. Load alongside figma-use whenever a task involves adding, editing, or inspecting animation on a node.
- figma:figma-use-slides: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the Slides context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

services:
  # ── Фронтовое приложение (PWA) ─────────────────────────────
  - type: web
    name: edu-app
    runtime: static
    buildCommand: yarn install --immutable && yarn build
    staticPublishPath: packages/edu-app/dist
    routes:
      - type: rewrite
        source: /*
        destination: /index.html
    headers:
      - path: /sw.js
        name: Cache-Control
        value: no-cache
      - path: /index.html
        name: Cache-Control
        value: no-cache
      - path: /manifest.webmanifest
        name: Cache-Control
        value: no-cache
    envVars:
      - key: NODE_VERSION
        value: "20"
      - key: VITE_API_BASE_URL
        value: https://kakoi-to-do-men.ru/api/v1

  # ── Админка (обычный SPA) ──────────────────────────────────
  - type: web
    name: edu-admin
    runtime: static
    buildCommand: yarn install --immutable && yarn build:admin
    staticPublishPath: packages/admin/dist
    routes:
      - type: rewrite
        source: /*
        destination: /index.html
    headers:
      - path: /index.html
        name: Cache-Control
        value: no-cache
    envVars:
      - key: NODE_VERSION
        value: "20"
      - key: VITE_API_BASE_URL
        value: https://kakoi-to-do-men.ru/api/v1
===RENDER END===

13	} from "../lib/types";

15	import { unwrapApiResponse } from "@edu-pwa/core";
16	
17	export const {
18	  useGenerateGuestTestQuery,
19	  useGenerateTestQuery,
20	  useLazyGenerateTestQuery,
21	  useGetGeneratedTestByIdQuery,
22	  useSubmitGuestTestAnswersMutation,
23	  useSubmitTestAnswersMutation,

28	  useLazyGetTestAiExplanationQuery,
29	} = baseApi.injectEndpoints({
30	  endpoints: builder => ({
31	    generateGuestTest: builder.query<GeneratedTest, GenerateGuestTestBody>({
32	      query: body => ({
33	        url: "/tests/generate-guest",

35	        body,
36	      }),
37	      transformResponse: unwrapApiResponse,

39	    }),

41	      query: body => ({
42	        url: "/tests/generate",

45	      }),

48	    }),
49	    // Достать ранее сгенерированный тест по id (напр. восстановление после рефреша

51	    getGeneratedTestById: builder.query<GeneratedTest, string>({
52	      query: id => ({
53	        url: `/tests/${id}`,
54	        method: "GET",
55	      }),
56	      transformResponse: unwrapApiResponse,
57	      providesTags: ["GuestTest"],
58	    }),
59	    /** Гостевой submit: результат + детальный разбор + темы для повторения сразу (без AI). */
60	    submitGuestTestAnswers: builder.mutation<GuestSubmitResponse, SubmitTestAnswersBody>({
61	      query: body => ({
62	        url: "/tests/submit-guest",
63	        method: "POST",
64	        body,
65	      }),
66	      transformResponse: unwrapApiResponse,
67	      invalidatesTags: ["GuestTest"],
68	    }),
69	    /** Авторизованный submit: ЛЁГКИЙ ответ (только result + testHistoryId). Разбор/AI — лениво. */
70	    submitTestAnswers: builder.mutation<SubmitTestAnswersResponse, SubmitTestAnswersBody>({
71	      query: body => ({
72	        url: "/tests/submit",
73	        method: "POST",
74	        body,
75	      }),
76	      transformResponse: unwrapApiResponse,
77	      invalidatesTags: ["AuthorizedTest", "User"],
78	    }),
79	    claimGuestTest: builder.mutation<GuestSubmitResponse, ClaimGuestTestBody>({
80	      query: body => ({
81	        url: "/tests/claim-guest",
82	        method: "POST",
83	        body,
84	      }),
85	      transformResponse: unwrapApiResponse,
86	      invalidatesTags: ["AuthorizedTest", "User"],
87	    }),
88	    /** Детальный разбор прошлого теста по записи истории. */
89	    getTestBreakdown: builder.query<TestBreakdownResponse, string>({
90	      query: testHistoryId => ({
91	        url: `/users/me/tests/${testHistoryId}/breakdown`,
92	        method: "GET",
93	      }),
94	      transformResponse: unwrapApiResponse,
95	      providesTags: (_r, _e, id) => [{ type: "AuthorizedTest", id }],
96	    }),

101	        method: "GET",
102	      }),
103	      transformResponse: unwrapApiResponse,
104	      providesTags: (_r, _e, id) => [{ type: "AuthorizedTest", id: `${id}:ai` }],

I have everything I need. Here is the structured report.

---

# Report: ЕНТ test-prep — production frontend & node-test start flow

## A. Which frontend is production? → `edu-pwa/packages/edu-app` (HIGH confidence)

**Deploy config is decisive:**
- `/Users/madiever/edu-pwa/render.yaml:3-30` defines a Render static `web` service **`edu-app`** that builds `packages/edu-app/dist` (`yarn install --immutable && yarn build`), SPA-rewrites `/* → /index.html`, and injects `VITE_API_BASE_URL = https://kakoi-to-do-men.ru/api/v1`. A second service `edu-admin` builds `packages/admin/dist`. This is the deployed student PWA + admin.
- `/Users/madiever/GenTestMVP` has **no** `render.yaml`, nginx config, or Dockerfile. Its only deploy descriptor is `ecosystem.config.js` (PM2), which runs **only the backend** (`edu-ai-api` → `server/dist/server.js` on :5111) plus `whatsapp-bot` and `telegram-bot`. The `client` is **not** in the PM2 config, and `server/src` does not `express.static`-serve any `client/dist` (grep empty).
- `GenTestMVP/README.md:34` documents the client as dev-only: `cd client && npm run dev # SPA — http://localhost:5173`.

**So the topology is:** `GenTestMVP/server` is the production **backend** (api at `kakoi-to-do-men.ru/api/v1`); `edu-pwa/packages/edu-app` is the production **student frontend** (Render) that calls that backend; `GenTestMVP/client` is the **legacy combined SPA** bundled in the backend repo.

**On recency** (note, not decisive): both are still receiving commits. `edu-pwa` HEAD = `f335cd7 2026-06-23`; `GenTestMVP` HEAD = `b6fa126 2026-06-23`. ## C. Roadmap зачёт (completion credit) in edu-app

The node↔test link in production is carried by **`roadmapNodeId`**, sent on BOTH generate and submit. (`saveRoadmapContext`/`roadmapContext` do **not** exist in edu-app — grep empty; those are legacy-only.)

**On START** — `nodeId` goes in the generate body (`useAuthorizedTestSession.ts:37`). Reducer stores it as `roadmapNodeId` in flow state (`testFlowState/index.ts:79`).

**On FINISH/SUBMIT** — `/Users/madiever/edu-pwa/packages/edu-app/src/features/test/lib/hooks/useAuthorizedTestSubmit.ts:38-44`:
```ts
const { testId, questions, answers, roadmapNodeId: rNode, roadmapSessionId: rSession } = flow;
const body = buildSubmitTestAnswersBody(testId, questions, answers, rNode, rSession);
const response = await submitTestAnswers(body).unwrap();
```
- `buildSubmitTestAnswersBody` (`entities/test/lib/types.ts:324-340`) emits `{ testId, roadmapNodeId?, roadmapSessionId?, answers[] }` — each id is **omitted when empty**.
- Endpoint: `submitTestAnswers` → `POST /tests/submit` (`entities/test/model/index.ts:70-78`), invalidates `["AuthorizedTest","User"]`.
- The submit response type already carries roadmap progress back: `SubmitTestAnswersResponse.roadmap` and `trialTopicMastery` (`entities/test/lib/types.ts:189-195`). The "освоен/mastered" decision is **server-side**, keyed on `roadmapNodeId`.

**`roadmapSessionId` is plumbed but inert in edu-app.** It exists in `PersistedTest` (`types.ts:138`), flow state, and the submit body, but is **never populated client-side**: `useAuthorizedTestAcquisition.ts:53-60` calls `applyRemoteGuestTestToState` passing only `roadmapNodeId` (no `roadmapSessionId`), so it stays `""` and is dropped from the submit body. (Contrast with the legacy client, which DOES generate it — Section D.)

**Persistence (resume only, not зачёт):** `usePersistedTest(subjectId, bookId, roadmapNodeId)` (`useAuthorizedTestSession.ts:20`) writes a `PersistedTest` to **sessionStorage** under key prefix `edu-test-` (`TEST_STORAGE_KEY_PREFIX`, `types.ts:286`; concrete key form `edu-test-${bookId}-${_id}` per `widgets/trialTest/ui/TrialTestLauncherWidget.tsx:32`). `roadmapNodeId`/`roadmapSessionId` are included in the payload (`useAuthorizedTestSession.ts:64-65`) so an in-progress test survives refresh and still submits with the node link.

After submit, `RoadmapTestResultSheet` shows the score / `pointsDelta` (`RoadmapTestResultStats`, `types.ts:114-119`); leaving navigates back to `ROUTES.ROADMAP` (`RoadmapTestPage.tsx:54-57`), where `getPersonalRoadmap` (`entities/roadmap/model/index.ts:23-33`, `GET /roadmaps/personal?subjectId=…`) re-fetches advanced progress.

## D. Legacy `GenTestMVP/client` flow (confirming it's redundant)

`/Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/client/src/pages/user/KnowledgeMapPage.tsx:128-177` — `handleStartTestForNode(nodeId)`:
- Blocks if `knowledgeMapTestBlocked` (`:131-137`); resolves `bookId`/`chapterId` (`:144-151`).
- **Generates a client-side session id** (`:153`): `const sessionId = \`roadmap-${subjectId}-${nodeId}-${Date.now()}\``.
- Calls **`saveRoadmapContext({ subjectId, nodeId, nodeTitle, sessionId, bookId, chapterId, fullBook })`** (`:156-164`) → `client/src/utils/session.ts:84-86`, which writes to **sessionStorage** under `ROADMAP_CONTEXT_KEY`.
- Navigates to `/user/test/start` with router `state` `{ subjectId, bookId, chapterId, fullBook, roadmapNodeId, roadmapNodeTitle, roadmapSessionId }` (`:166-176`).

`/Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/client/src/pages/user/TestStartPage.tsx:163-196` builds the payload (`{subjectId, bookId, chapterId, fullBook, testProfile, topicFocus?, roadmapNodeId?, questionCount?}`, `:163-175`) and calls `testApi.generateTest(payload)` → **`POST /tests/generate`** (`client/src/api/test.api.ts:18`, axios). It then re-saves the roadmap context (`:185-195`). Submit uses `testApi.submitTest` → `POST /tests/submit` (`test.api.ts:23`).

**Conclusion:** the legacy client hits the **exact same backend endpoints** (`/tests/generate`, `/tests/submit`) as production edu-app. Differences are purely client architecture: axios + sessionStorage `roadmapContext` + a **client-generated `roadmapSessionId`**, vs edu-app's RTK Query + reducer with `roadmapNodeId` only (`roadmapSessionId` inert). Functionally equivalent at the API layer → the legacy client is a redundant frontend and is not part of the Render deploy. Write your code against the **edu-app** flow in Sections B/C and the shared backend `/tests/generate` (+`/tests/submit`).

In edu-app there is **no `/test/:id` route**. `ROUTES.TEST(id)` = `/test/${id}` (`paths.ts:15`) is used by `RoadmapScreenWidget.tsx:58` ("Перейти к тесту" in the node drawer) and `widgets/dashboardHomePanel/ui/DashboardHomePanel.tsx:88`, but the router only defines `/test/roadmap/:subjectId/:bookId/:nodeId`, `/test/trial/:bookId/:subjectId`, and a `/test/*` NotFound (`config.tsx:103-122, 308-345`). So those two `ROUTES.TEST(nodeId)` navigations appear to land on NotFound — the only verified-working node-test launch is the **lesson screen → `ROUTES.TEST_ROADMAP`** path (Section B).
